name: Subscription Price Drift Monitor (powered by easybits)

parameters.content:
## 📩 Trigger: New Labeled Email
Watches Gmail for new emails carrying the `new invoice` label. Whenever Tom labels a receipt, the workflow picks it up on the next poll (every minute). PDF attachments are downloaded automatically.

## 📄 Prepare Email Body
This step takes the email's subject, sender, date, and body and turns them into a clean format that easybits can read. Pure JavaScript, no external services needed.

## 🤖 Extract Receipt Details from Email Body
Sends the email body PDF to the **easybits** Extractor pipeline. easybits reads it and returns 6 structured fields: vendor, amount, currency, plan name, billing period, and invoice date.

## 🔗 Reattach the Original Attachment
The Extractor drops attachments after processing. This step reunites the extracted data with the original PDF attachment (if there was one) so the next step can check whether one exists.

## 🔀 Does the Email Have a PDF Attachment?
Some receipts have all the info in the email body. Others come as a PDF attachment with a short note in the email. This branch checks if there's a PDF attached and, if so, sends it through a second round of extraction.

## 📎 Prepare the PDF Attachment
Saves the email body's extraction results to the side (so they don't get lost), then picks out the first PDF attachment and hands it to the next step for a second round of extraction.

## 🤖 Extract Receipt Details from Attachment
Sends the PDF attachment to the same **easybits** pipeline. Returns the same 6 fields again – this time from the attachment's content. Useful as a backup in case the email body was light on details.

## 🧮 Combine Both Extractions
If both the email body and the attachment were processed, this step merges the two – preferring whatever the email body said, and falling back to the attachment for any missing fields. The result: one clean row ready for comparison and storage.

## 🔍 Check if This Receipt is Already in the Ledger
Searches the Google Sheet for a row with the same email ID. This is the safety net: if Tom accidentally labels the same email twice, the workflow won't add it again.

## 🔀 New Receipt or Duplicate?
Routes the receipt down one of two paths – either it's new (add it to the ledger) or it's already there (send a heads-up email and skip).

## 📊 Look Up This Vendor's History
Fetches all previous receipts for the same vendor from the Google Sheet. These rows form the rolling baseline that the next step uses to detect price changes.

## 🧮 Compare Against the Rolling Baseline
Averages the last 3 receipts for this vendor and compares the new amount against that average. Calculates how much the price went up (or down) in dollars and percent. Also notices if the subscription plan changed (often the *why* behind a price change). If this is the first receipt for the vendor, no comparison is made – it just gets logged.

## 🔀 Did the Price Go Up?
If the new amount is higher than the rolling average → send an alert. If the price stayed the same or dropped → log it silently and move on.

## 🚨 Send Price Drift Alert
Sends Tom an email summarizing what changed: the new amount, the rolling baseline, the dollar and percent increase, and whether the plan changed. The subject line shows the percent increase at a glance.

## ✅ Add Receipt to the Ledger
Appends the receipt as a new row in the **Subscription Ledger** Google Sheet. This happens whether the price went up, down, or stayed the same – every new receipt becomes part of the rolling baseline for future comparisons.

## 🏷️ Remove Label
Strips the `new invoice` label from the email so it isn't processed again on the next poll.

## 📧 Send Duplicate Notice
Sends Tom a short email letting him know this receipt was already in the ledger and wasn't added again. Keeps him in the loop without cluttering the Sheet.

## 🏷️ Remove Label
Strips the `new invoice` label so the duplicate email isn't picked up again.

# 💸 Subscription Price Drift Monitor (powered by easybits)

## What This Workflow Does
Tom's AI tool subscriptions kept quietly getting more expensive – small price bumps that he never noticed until his card statement crept up by 30% over six months. This workflow watches Gmail for new subscription receipts that he labels `new invoice`, reads the vendor and amount with **easybits**, compares against the rolling average of his last 3 receipts from the same vendor, and sends him an email alert when the price has gone up.

Decreases and no-change receipts are logged silently. Only price increases trigger an alert. This is the companion to the **Subscription Baseline Seeder** workflow – run that one first to seed the historical receipts.

## How It Works
1. Tom labels a new receipt email with `new invoice` in Gmail
2. The email body is converted into a PDF the **easybits** Extractor can read
3. easybits pulls out vendor, amount, currency, plan, billing period, and date
4. If the email also has a PDF receipt attached, easybits reads that too – and any missing fields in the body get filled in from the attachment
5. The workflow checks whether this receipt was already processed (duplicate guard)
6. It looks up the vendor's history in the Google Sheet and averages the last 3 receipts
7. If the new amount is higher than the average → Tom gets a price drift alert email
8. The receipt is added to the ledger either way, and the Gmail label is removed

## Setup Guide

### 1. Run the Subscription Baseline Seeder First
This workflow only works once your **Subscription Ledger** Google Sheet has historical receipts in it. Set up and run the **Subscription Baseline Seeder** workflow first to seed your last 2–3 receipts per vendor.

### 2. Create the Gmail Label
In Gmail, create a label called `new invoice` (lowercase, with the space). Use it for incoming subscription receipts you want monitored.

### 3. Use the Same easybits Pipeline
This workflow uses the same `Subscription Receipt` Extractor pipeline as the Baseline Seeder. No new pipeline needed – just paste your existing **Pipeline ID** and **API Key** into both Extractor nodes.

### 4. Connect Credentials in n8n
1. **Gmail Trigger** – connect your Gmail OAuth and select the `new invoice` label
2. **easybits nodes** – paste your existing pipeline credentials into both Extractor nodes
3. **Google Sheets nodes** – connect your Google Sheets OAuth and point all three nodes at the **Subscription Ledger** sheet (the same one used by the Seeder)
4. **Gmail notification + label nodes** – confirm your Gmail OAuth is selected on all three Gmail action nodes

### 5. Test and Activate
Label one new receipt with `new invoice`. The workflow should:
1. Add it to the ledger
2. Send a price drift alert if the amount is higher than the vendor's recent average
3. Remove the Gmail label


Once that works, activate the workflow and label every new subscription receipt as it comes in.
